items.title: Driver in Christmas vehicle attack in Germany that killed 6 people sentenced to life in person
items.summary_auto: A Saudi doctor was sentenced to life in prison in Germany on ‌Friday for killing six people and injuring hundreds, after he rammed a rented BMW into crowds at a historic market in the eastern city of Magdeburg days before Christmas in 2024.

items.title: RBC fined $4.25 million over incorrect credit card statements
items.summary_auto: Between 2001 and 2024, the FCAC says RBC failed to transfer credits from deactivated credit card accounts to customers' new ones. This resulted in incorrect monthly credit card statements.

items.title: Remember the P.E.I. restaurant with $0 rent and ocean views? It finally found an operator
items.summary_auto: A restaurant at Prince Edward Island’s northernmost point has found a new operator for this summer, after a public search that included a $0-rent offer.

items.title: Europe on high alert as killer heat spreads, prompting alcohol ban and Pride parade delay in France
items.summary_auto: Health authorities across Europe were on high alert on Friday as a killer heat wave progressed across the continent, prompting alcohol bans and festival cancellations in France and cracking road surfaces open in Germany.

items.title: Christine Sinclair, Michael J. Fox promoted to Order of Canada's highest level
items.summary_auto: Recently appointed Gov. Gen. Louise Arbour on Friday added 56 new Canadians to what is considered the country's highest civilian honour.

items.title: Former Alberta doctor again ordered to stop ‘cancer coaching’ as appeal bid rejected
items.summary_auto: Alberta’s highest court has barred a former Edmonton physician from launching an appeal against court-ordered sanctions that prohibit him from practising medicine or implying he’s licensed to care for any patient in the province.

items.title: Serena Williams draws 20-year-old Maya Joint in first Wimbledon singles match in 4 years
items.summary_auto: Serena Williams will face an opponent less than half her age when she plays 20-year-old Maya Joint in the first round at Wimbledon for her first singles match in nearly four years.

items.title: Tears and champagne as Saskatoon runner finishes 1,500-km north-to-south run in memory of her mother
items.summary_auto: A Saskatoon teacher who just turned 38 — the same age her mother died from leukemia — marked the milestone by running 1,500 kilometres in a month, crossing Saskatchewan north to south. She reached the finish line at the Canada-U.S. border Thursday.

items.title: Maple Leafs or not, Yukon's Gavin McKenna set to make hockey history in NHL draft
items.summary_auto: McKenna is projected to be the first overall selection at this year's draft, which happens on Friday. That means the 18-year-old  could be headed to Toronto.

items.title: Alberta promising better care for cancer patients amid ongoing concerns about waits
items.summary_auto: The Alberta government says it is working to improve access to cancer care with a new 10-year strategic plan. It comes amid new cancer cases rising, health systems coming under increasing strain, and ongoing concerns over long waits for key cancer surgeries.

items.title: Russia reports large-scale Ukrainian drone attack across 12 regions
items.summary_auto: Russian air defences intercepted 660 Ukrainian drones in a major nighttime attack on 12 Russian regions as well as the Russia-held Crimean peninsula, the Black Sea and the Azov Sea, Russia's Defence Ministry said Friday.

items.title: We know violent and gory videos exist online. Teens are seeing more of them than you'd think
items.summary_auto: School fights. The Charlie Kirk killing. Violent encounters with police. A new survey of more than 1,000 teens across Canada offers a glimpse into the violent content they say they're seeing online — largely without seeking it out — and what young people want done about it.

items.title: 3 in 10 knee replacement surgeries in Manitoba met Canada's wait-time benchmark in 2025, data shows
items.summary_auto: Manitobans are facing long waits for knee replacement surgeries, as new data shows only about three in 10 procedures completed last year were done within Canada's six-month benchmark.

items.title: Lawsuit filed in case of polluting, derelict N.S. tug that cost $4.1M to clean up
items.summary_auto: The alleged owner of a derelict former tugboat that was leaking oil in a rural harbour along Nova Scotia’s Eastern Shore is being sued over cleanup and dismantling costs that the Canadian Coast Guard claims top $4.1 million.

items.title: He managed Jesuit finances for decades. Now he has to repay the Catholic order $8.8M in stolen funds
Iran and Egypt will headline Seattle's 'Pride Match.' Here's why that's controversial
items.summary_auto: Iran and Egypt's FIFA World Cup game on Friday is being marketed as the 'Pride Match' by Seattle organizers. It's not a decision made by FIFA, but that hasn't stopped a wave of controversy from clouding the buildup to the pivotal group-stage game.

items.title: Supergirl is almost impressively boring. Almost.
items.summary_auto: Written by first-time screenwriter Ana Nogueira and directed by action-newbie Craig Gillespie, it is frankly a shock how directionless this follow-up to the DCU's 2025 eclectically energetic Superman is.

items.title: Energy minister to name 5 priority power-line projects for federal backing today
items.summary_auto: The federal government is expected to announce on Friday that it will prioritize infrastructure upgrades that allow provinces to share excess electricity more cheaply and easily.

items.title: Northern Ontario leaders react to possible fast-tracking of nuclear waste repository
items.summary_auto: The federal government has announced Canada's proposed deep geological repository for nuclear waste in northwestern Ontario is possibly being listed as a project of national interest, which means the construction timeline for the $26-billion project could be accelerated under the Building Canada Act. The news is being welcomed by some leaders, who say the project is critical for the region’s economic revival.

items.title: Why Europe can't air condition its way out of extreme heat
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of people are dead from Europe’s heatwave, in a continent where air conditioning is not the norm. But while ACs may save lives, they can also make the problem worse.

items.title: B.C. police issue arrest warrant for former provincial correctional officer charged with breach of trust
Blue Jays' Ernie Clement into all-star game after receiving most American League fan votes
items.summary_auto: Toronto Blue Jays infielder Ernie Clement is heading to the MLB all-star game after finishing as the surprise top recipient of fan votes among American League players.

items.title: Heated Rivalry musical parody heads home to Canada, first stop: Toronto
items.summary_auto: Heated Rivalry: The Unauthorized Musical Parody is heading to Toronto for a limited run starting July 9, debuting just after Pride and during Fringe. Canadian director-producer Alan Kliffer says the show’s off-Broadway success started as a fandom-inspired idea to turn the hit Canadian TV series' romance into a musical parody. He hopes Toronto keeps the momentum going toward a full-scale Canadian production.

items.title: Carney announces fundraising campaign, design competition to restore 24 Sussex: source
Montreal shooting leads to national security investigation on ideological violence
items.summary_auto: A specialized national security unit within the RCMP has opened an investigation into ideologically motivated violent extremism after Monday's midday shooting in Montreal that left three people dead, including a police officer and a bystander.

items.title: 16 arrested in Calgary probe into South Asian extortion; all but one are out on bail
items.summary_auto: Calgary police say they are making progress in tackling extortion against members of the city's South Asian community. But they say they need the public's help and released mugshots of most of the suspects in an attempt to get it.

items.title: Venezuelan Montrealers anxiously await news of loved ones after deadly earthquakes
items.summary_auto: Montrealers of Venezuelan descent are living in uncertainty after two earthquakes struck north central Venezuela on Wednesday afternoon. The earthquakes, one a magnitude of 7.2 and the other 7.5, struck just one minute apart, causing widespread devastation and triggering the collapse of several buildings in the capital Caracas.

items.title: Canada 'at a disadvantage' in Iran but no plan to open embassy, Carney says
items.summary_auto: Canada is "at a disadvantage" in countries like Iran where it lacks a diplomatic presence, but his government is not looking to re-establish relations with Tehran, Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Thursday.

items.title: E-scooter injuries can be deadly, debilitating — and they're on the rise, Alberta doctors warn
items.summary_auto: The number of Albertans visiting emergency rooms and urgent care centres due to electric scooter incidents is growing steadily, with children’s hospitals seeing the sharpest increases.

items.title: King Charles won't live in Buckingham Palace after completion of £369M renovation
items.summary_auto: Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after the completion of a 10-year, 369-million-pound ($692 million Cdn) refurbishment program as the monarchy seeks to increase public access to the historic building that has been the centre of royal life for almost 200 years.

items.title: Scooter-riding bank robber evades police in back-to-back stickups in Dawson Creek, B.C.
items.summary_auto: RCMP say although the two robberies took place four days and 170 metres apart, it is not clear if they are connected or if there are two scooter-riding bank robbers at large.

items.title: Did Canada luck out with its World Cup playoff matchup?
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter assesses Canada's chances against South Africa in the World Cup knockout stage this Sunday at 3 p.m. ET.

items.title: 47 Quebec physicians recruited to N.B. health-care system since October
items.summary_auto: New Brunswick has granted 47 licenses to Quebec physicians since October 2025 after doctors were at odds with the Quebec provincial government. Vitalité Health Network confirmed it received 19 of those physicians, with nine already beginning the job.

items.title: Ship evacuation in Strait of Hormuz paused following attack on cargo vessel
items.summary_auto: The UN International Maritime Organization (IMO) paused its program to shepherd ships and seafarers through the Strait of ‌Hormuz on Thursday after a cargo ship reported a suspected attack, reigniting fears over a preliminary deal to end the Iran war.

items.title: Alberta man charged with sex crimes involving minors while out on bail
items.summary_auto: Grande Prairie RCMP urges potential victims to come forward as Joseph Kidd has received numerous charges relating to sexual offences. Police have identified four alleged victims so far, ranging from 12 to 18 years old.

items.title: Residents northwest of Pemberton, B.C., ordered to leave as glacial lake threatens to burst
items.summary_auto: Residents of a small community northeast of Pemberton, B.C., have been ordered to leave immediately due to the imminent risk of flooding from a nearby glacial lake.

items.title: Carney says he talked about Iran, NATO in wide-ranging security call with Trump
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says a lengthy conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump went well beyond NATO, covering Iran, Middle East security, Arctic defence and Canada's military spending plans. Carney also predicted Canada will hit four per cent of GDP spent on defence by 2029, ahead of NATO's planned review.
